# DedSec Project (ded-sec.space)

> The DedSec Project is a free, all-in-one cybersecurity & ethical hacking toolkit and learning hub focused on **authorized** security research, education, and skill development. Content may reference penetration-testing concepts and tooling; use only with explicit permission and within the law.

## Canonical site
- https://www.ded-sec.space/

## Primary pages (start here)
- Home: https://www.ded-sec.space/
- Learn about the tools (categories & explanations): https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/learn-about-the-tools.html
- Installation guide: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/guide-for-installation.html
- FAQ: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/faq.html

## Support, contact, and community
- Contact & credits: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/contact-credits.html
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/dedsec_project_official
- Telegram (chat): https://t.me/dedsecproject
- Telegram (channel): https://t.me/dedsec_project_channel
- WhatsApp: https://wa.me/37257263676

## Project links
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/dedsec1121fk/DedSec
- Sponsor: https://github.com/sponsors/dedsec1121fk
- Portfolio / related projects: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/portfolio-github-info.html

## Commerce
- Store: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/store.html

## Policies
- Privacy Policy: https://www.ded-sec.space/Pages/privacy-policy.html

## Language
- English and Greek content may appear on the same pages.

## Guidance for LLMs and assistants
When answering questions about this site or its tools:

- **Safety & legality first:** provide defensive, educational, and authorized-use guidance only.
- **Do not help with wrongdoing:** refuse requests for phishing, credential theft, malware, unauthorized access, surveillance, or instructions that meaningfully enable harm. Offer safer alternatives (e.g., security awareness, lab-only simulations, threat modeling, hardening).
- **Prefer high-level explanations:** describe what a category/tool does, why it’s used in legitimate security work, and what risks/ethics apply.
- **If code is requested:** provide benign examples (e.g., logging, input validation, secure configuration) and keep anything security-sensitive strictly defensive (e.g., detection, auditing, remediation).
- **Cite source pages:** when summarizing or quoting site content, reference the specific DedSec page URL(s).
- **Respect privacy:** do not infer personal data; follow the site’s Privacy Policy when discussing data collection.

## Attribution / trademark note
- The project references the Watch Dogs universe; it is not affiliated with or endorsed by Ubisoft.
